Title: Plot weight-for-age on WHO growth chart for an infant under 24 months
Preconditions: Logged in clinician; patient is a 12-month-old infant with at least 3 recorded weight observations
Test_Steps: 1. Open the infant patient record | 2. Navigate to the Growth Chart view | 3. Select WHO weight-for-age standard for the patient sex | 4. Confirm plotted points
Test_Data: Patient=Baby A (DOB makes age 12mo); Sex=Female; Weights=7.0kg@6mo,8.2kg@9mo,9.1kg@12mo
Expected_Result: Three weight points are plotted on WHO weight-for-age (girls 0-2y) chart at correct ages with percentile/z-score curves visible

Title: Switch from WHO (0-2y) to CDC (2-20y) chart at the age-band boundary
Preconditions: Patient has growth data spanning the 24-month boundary
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient turning 24 months | 2. Open Growth Chart | 3. Observe chart selection at 23mo vs 24mo | 4. Verify reference standard switch
Expected_Result: System uses WHO for ages under 24mo and CDC (or configured standard) for 24mo and over; transition occurs exactly at 24 completed months

Title: Calculate weight-for-age percentile and z-score for a known reference value
Preconditions: Patient with sex and exact age in days; reference LMS tables available
Test_Steps: 1. Record a weight observation with exact datetime | 2. Open Growth Chart percentile readout | 3. Compare computed percentile/z-score against reference LMS value
Test_Data: Sex=Male; Age=730 days; Weight=12.2kg; Expected z near WHO median
Expected_Result: Computed z-score and percentile match the LMS-derived reference within tolerance (z +/-0.05); median weight maps to ~50th percentile

Title: Reject percentile calculation when patient sex is unknown
Preconditions: Patient record has sex recorded as Unknown
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with Unknown sex | 2. Record weight | 3. Open Growth Chart | 4. Attempt percentile readout
Expected_Result: System does not display a misleading percentile; it shows a clear message that sex is required for percentile/z-score and suppresses the readout

Title: Flag severe acute malnutrition when weight-for-height z-score below -3
Preconditions: Patient with valid length/height and weight; SAM threshold configured at z=-3
Test_Steps: 1. Record length and weight | 2. Open growth assessment | 3. Observe WFH/WFL z-score and nutritional flag
Test_Data: Sex=Female; Length=80cm; Weight=8.0kg producing WFL z below -3
Expected_Result: System computes WFL z-score below -3 and raises a severe acute malnutrition flag/alert for clinician action

Title: Decision table for nutritional status banding by z-score
Preconditions: Growth assessment configured with WHO z-score bands
Test_Steps: 1. Enter weight/height combinations producing z in each band | 2. Read resulting nutritional category
Expected_Result: Each z-score maps to the correct WHO category: <-3 severe wasting, -3 to -2 moderate, -2 to +2 normal, +2 to +3 overweight, >+3 obese

Title: Detect implausible growth velocity (weight drop across visits)
Preconditions: Patient with prior weight; new weight entered far below expected
Test_Steps: 1. Record weight much lower than prior visit | 2. Save observation | 3. Observe validation/alert
Expected_Result: System raises an implausible-value or growth-faltering warning prompting verification before save; value not silently accepted

Title: Length vs height measurement method affects chart selection
Preconditions: Patient near 24 months where method matters
Test_Steps: 1. Record measurement with method=recumbent length | 2. Record with method=standing height | 3. Observe which reference applied
Expected_Result: System applies length-based reference for recumbent and height-based reference for standing, accounting for ~0.7cm method difference per WHO guidance

Title: Plot head circumference-for-age for infant
Preconditions: Infant patient with head circumference observation recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record head circumference | 2. Open Growth Chart HC-for-age | 3. Verify plotted point and percentile
Expected_Result: HC-for-age point plots correctly with percentile/z-score relative to WHO HC reference

Title: Macrocephaly alert when HC z-score above +2
Preconditions: HC reference loaded; alert threshold at z=+2
Test_Steps: 1. Record large head circumference | 2. Save | 3. Observe macrocephaly/alert flag
Test_Data: Sex=Female; Age=3mo; HC=44cm producing z above +2
Expected_Result: System flags HC above +2 z-score as macrocephaly warranting review; below threshold produces no flag

Title: Calculate weight-based dose for a pediatric drug order
Preconditions: Clinician ordering for a child with a current recorded weight
Test_Steps: 1. Open drug order for pediatric patient | 2. Select drug with mg/kg dosing | 3. Enter dose rate | 4. Verify computed total dose
Test_Data: Drug=Amoxicillin; Rate=25 mg/kg/day divided BID; Weight=14kg
Expected_Result: System computes total daily dose 350mg/day, 175mg per dose, and displays mg/kg basis for verification

Title: Block weight-based dose when no recent weight on record
Preconditions: Pediatric patient with no weight recorded in configured lookback window
Test_Steps: 1. Open weight-based drug order | 2. Attempt to calculate dose | 3. Observe behavior
Test_Data: Drug=Gentamicin; Weight=none recorded in last 30 days
Expected_Result: System prevents auto-calculation and requires a current weight, warning that dosing cannot proceed without it

Title: Enforce maximum (adult cap) dose for weight-based order
Preconditions: Heavy adolescent whose weight-based calc exceeds adult maximum
Test_Steps: 1. Order weight-based drug for adolescent | 2. Enter mg/kg rate | 3. Observe computed vs capped dose
Test_Data: Drug=Paracetamol; Rate=15 mg/kg; Weight=70kg gives 1050mg; Max single=1000mg
Expected_Result: System caps the computed dose at the configured adult maximum and alerts that the cap was applied

Title: Pairwise dosing across drug, age band and weight inputs
Preconditions: Multiple pediatric patients across age bands with valid weights
Test_Steps: 1. Place weight-based orders for each pair | 2. Verify computed dose and unit handling
Expected_Result: Each combination produces correct unit-consistent dose; neonatal and adolescent edge inputs handled without rounding errors

Title: Reject negative or zero weight in dosing calculator
Preconditions: Pediatric drug order open with manual weight override field
Test_Steps: 1. Enter weight of 0 then a negative value | 2. Attempt calculation
Expected_Result: System rejects 0 and negative weight with validation error; no dose is computed

Title: Rounding to available dose form for liquid suspension
Preconditions: Drug available as suspension with fixed concentration
Test_Steps: 1. Compute weight-based dose | 2. Convert to volume using concentration | 3. Verify rounded volume
Expected_Result: System converts 175mg to 3.5mL and rounds sensibly to a measurable volume, showing both mg and mL

Title: Validate heart rate against neonate normal range
Preconditions: Active visit; patient is a neonate (0-28 days)
Test_Steps: 1. Open vitals form | 2. Enter heart rate at low, normal, high bounds | 3. Save and observe flags
Test_Data: Age=10 days; HR values=70,140,205 bpm (neonate ref ~100-205)
Expected_Result: HR 70 flagged as low (bradycardia), 140 normal, 205 at upper bound accepted; flags reflect neonatal not adult ranges

Title: Respiratory rate range shifts by age band
Preconditions: Vitals form supports age-specific reference ranges
Test_Steps: 1. Enter same RR=40 for neonate, infant, school-age, adolescent | 2. Observe normal/abnormal flag per band
Expected_Result: RR=40 normal for neonate/young infant but flagged high for school-age and adolescent per pediatric RR norms

Title: Blood pressure interpretation requires height percentile for children
Preconditions: Child patient with recorded height and sex
Test_Steps: 1. Record systolic/diastolic BP | 2. System looks up BP percentile by age, sex, height percentile | 3. Observe classification
Expected_Result: BP is classified (normal/elevated/HTN stage) using age-sex-height percentile tables, not a fixed adult cutoff

Title: Temperature unit conversion does not corrupt fever flag
Preconditions: Vitals form configured for Celsius with optional Fahrenheit entry
Test_Steps: 1. Enter 100.4F | 2. Confirm stored Celsius | 3. Observe fever flag
Expected_Result: Value stored as 38.0C, fever flag raised at pediatric threshold; round-trip conversion introduces no drift

Title: Oxygen saturation low threshold alert for infant
Preconditions: Active visit on infant; SpO2 captured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter SpO2 values at boundary | 2. Observe critical alert
Expected_Result: Values below configured pediatric SpO2 threshold raise a critical hypoxemia alert; boundary value handled per configured operator

Title: Register a newborn with estimated DOB and minimal demographics
Preconditions: Registration clerk logged in; mother present
Test_Steps: 1. Start patient registration | 2. Set DOB as exact birth date | 3. Enter name/sex | 4. Capture birth weight | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Newborn registered with identifier generated; age displays in days; birth weight stored as initial growth point

Title: Register newborn with unknown/unnamed status (Baby of)
Preconditions: Registration before the infant is formally named
Test_Steps: 1. Register infant | 2. Use placeholder given name convention | 3. Save | 4. Later edit to add formal name
Expected_Result: System accepts placeholder name, allows later update without losing identifier or linked records

Title: Reject future date of birth on newborn registration
Preconditions: Registration form open
Test_Steps: 1. Enter DOB in the future | 2. Attempt save
Expected_Result: System rejects future DOB with validation error; record not created

Title: Capture gestational age and flag prematurity
Preconditions: Newborn registration supports gestational age field
Test_Steps: 1. Register newborn | 2. Enter gestational age in weeks | 3. Observe prematurity classification
Expected_Result: System records gestational age and flags preterm (<37 weeks); term infant produces no preterm flag

Title: Corrected age used for growth plotting of preterm infant
Preconditions: Preterm infant registered with gestational age under 37 weeks
Test_Steps: 1. Open growth chart for preterm infant | 2. Toggle corrected age | 3. Observe plotting shift
Expected_Result: Growth points plot at corrected age (chronological minus prematurity) when corrected-age mode enabled, per AAP guidance

Title: Link a newborn to mother via parent-child relationship
Preconditions: Mother and newborn both registered as patients
Test_Steps: 1. Open newborn record | 2. Add relationship Parent/Child to mother | 3. Save | 4. Verify reciprocal link
Expected_Result: Relationship saved bidirectionally; mother shows as parent on infant and infant as child on mother

Title: Capture guardian/caregiver for minor with non-parent caregiver
Preconditions: Minor patient registered; guardian is not a biological parent
Test_Steps: 1. Add a Caregiver/Guardian relationship | 2. Record guardian contact | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Guardian relationship and contact recorded; available for consent and contact workflows

Title: Prevent self-referential or circular parent-child relationship
Preconditions: Patient record open in relationships editor
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to set patient as own parent | 2. Attempt to set child as parent of its own parent | 3. Observe validation
Test_Data: Patient A as parent of A; A child of B and B child of A
Expected_Result: System rejects self-referential and detectable circular relationships with validation error

Title: Guardian consent required for minor before sensitive action
Preconditions: Minor patient; guardian relationship recorded; consent workflow configured
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt a consent-gated action on minor | 2. System checks guardian consent | 3. Observe enforcement
Expected_Result: System requires recorded guardian consent for the minor before completing the action; blocks if absent

Title: Link immunization record to age-based recommended schedule
Preconditions: Pediatric patient with DOB; immunization schedule loaded
Test_Steps: 1. Open immunization view | 2. Record an administered vaccine | 3. Observe schedule status (due/given/overdue)
Expected_Result: Administered dose marks the schedule slot as given; subsequent due dates recalculated from DOB

Title: Flag overdue vaccine based on age and schedule
Preconditions: Patient older than the recommended age for an undelivered dose
Test_Steps: 1. Open immunization schedule | 2. Identify a dose past its due window | 3. Observe overdue status
Test_Data: Vaccine=MMR; RecommendedAge=9-12mo; PatientAge=18mo with no MMR
Expected_Result: System marks MMR as overdue; doses within window show due; future doses show upcoming

Title: Enforce minimum interval between vaccine doses
Preconditions: Patient with a prior dose recorded recently
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to record next dose before minimum interval | 2. Observe validation
Test_Data: Vaccine=Pentavalent; Dose1=today; attempt Dose2 in 2 weeks (min interval 4wk)
Expected_Result: System warns/blocks invalid early dose violating the minimum interval per schedule rules

Title: Represent immunization as FHIR Immunization resource
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint enabled; vaccine administration recorded
Test_Steps: 1. GET Immunization for patient via FHIR API | 2. Validate fields
Expected_Result: Response returns Immunization with patient reference, vaccineCode (CVX or configured system), occurrenceDateTime and status completed

Title: Expose pediatric weight observation via FHIR with LOINC code
Preconditions: FHIR enabled; weight observation recorded for child
Test_Steps: 1. GET Observation for body weight via FHIR | 2. Validate code and value/unit
Expected_Result: Observation returns LOINC 29463-7 body weight, UCUM unit kg, subject reference and effectiveDateTime

Title: Validate body length/height Observation uses correct LOINC for age
Preconditions: FHIR enabled; recumbent length recorded for infant
Test_Steps: 1. GET length Observation | 2. Verify LOINC distinguishes length vs height
Expected_Result: Length observation uses appropriate LOINC (body length 8306-3 vs height 8302-2) with UCUM cm

Title: Send pediatric vitals via HL7v2 ORU and verify OBX units
Preconditions: HL7v2 interface configured; vitals captured
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger ORU^R01 for pediatric vitals | 2. Inspect OBX segments for value/units
Expected_Result: OBX segments carry correct OBX-5 values and OBX-6 units; pediatric values transmit without truncation

Title: Newborn registration emits HL7 ADT^A28/A04 with correct DOB and sex
Preconditions: ADT interface configured; newborn registered
Test_Steps: 1. Register newborn | 2. Capture outbound ADT message | 3. Inspect PID-7 (DOB) and PID-8 (sex)
Expected_Result: ADT message contains correct PID-7 birth date with time precision and PID-8 administrative sex

Title: Age display switches units across day/month/year boundaries
Preconditions: Patients of varied ages available
Test_Steps: 1. Open records at 5 days, 20 months, 3 years | 2. Observe age rendering
Expected_Result: Age shows in days for neonate, months for infant/toddler, years for older child per display rules; no negative or zero-day errors

Title: Pediatric/adult workflow routing at age threshold
Preconditions: System configured with pediatric cutoff (e.g., under 18)
Test_Steps: 1. Open patients at 17y, 18y, 19y | 2. Observe which pediatric features (growth chart, weight dosing default) are offered
Expected_Result: Patients under cutoff get pediatric features; at and above cutoff default to adult workflows; boundary handled consistently

Title: Growth chart accessibility for screen reader and keyboard users
Preconditions: Patient with growth data; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to growth chart via keyboard only | 2. Inspect chart for text alternative/data table | 3. Verify focus order
Expected_Result: Chart exposes an accessible data table or alt text of plotted values; reachable and operable via keyboard with logical focus order

Title: Audit log records weight-based dose calculation and overrides
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; clinician places weight-based order
Test_Steps: 1. Place weight-based order | 2. Override computed dose | 3. Inspect audit log
Expected_Result: Audit log captures user, timestamp, computed dose, weight used and manual override with reason

Title: Database stores growth observations with units and precision
Preconditions: DB read access; growth observations recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Query obs table for weight/length | 2. Verify value, units concept and value precision
Expected_Result: Stored numeric values retain entered precision and correct unit concept; no implicit rounding/truncation in persistence

Title: Vitals state transition from draft to signed encounter
Preconditions: Clinician with active visit and vitals form
Test_Steps: 1. Start vitals form (draft) | 2. Save as incomplete | 3. Complete and sign | 4. Attempt edit after sign
Expected_Result: State transitions follow draft->saved->signed; post-sign edits create amendment rather than silent overwrite

Title: Immunization schedule state transitions due to overdue to given
Preconditions: Pediatric patient with upcoming vaccine slot
Test_Steps: 1. Observe slot Upcoming | 2. Age past due date -> Due then Overdue | 3. Administer -> Given
Expected_Result: Slot transitions Upcoming->Due->Overdue->Given correctly; once Given it cannot revert to Due

Title: Multiple birth (twins) registered with distinct identifiers and birth order
Preconditions: Mother of twins; registration supports multiple birth
Test_Steps: 1. Register Twin A | 2. Register Twin B | 3. Set birth order | 4. Link both to mother
Test_Data: TwinA order=1; TwinB order=2; same mother
Expected_Result: Both twins get unique identifiers, distinct birth order, and parent-child links to the same mother without collision

Title: Stale weight warning when recorded weight is older than threshold
Preconditions: Pediatric patient whose latest weight predates the freshness window
Test_Steps: 1. Open weight-based order | 2. System evaluates weight age | 3. Observe stale-weight handling
Test_Data: LatestWeight age=45 days; Freshness window=30 days
Expected_Result: System warns the weight is stale and prompts re-measurement before relying on it for dosing

Title: Adolescent confidentiality restricts guardian visibility of sensitive data
Preconditions: Adolescent patient; confidential category data present; guardian portal access
Test_Steps: 1. Mark a record category confidential for adolescent | 2. Access via guardian context | 3. Observe filtering
Expected_Result: Confidential adolescent data is withheld from guardian view per minor confidentiality policy while routine data remains visible

Title: Exploratory probing of growth chart with sparse and clustered data
Preconditions: Patients with edge data patterns
Test_Steps: 1. Open chart with single point | 2. Open chart with many same-day points | 3. Open chart with gaps | 4. Observe rendering
Test_Data: Datasets=1 point, 10 same-day points, multi-year gaps
Expected_Result: Chart renders gracefully for sparse, clustered and gapped data without overlap errors, crashes or misordered points

Title: Pairwise vitals validation across age band, vital sign and value position
Preconditions: Vitals form with age-specific ranges configured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter vital combinations across bands at low/normal/high | 2. Observe flagging
Expected_Result: Each pair flags correctly per the age-band reference; no cross-band leakage of adult ranges into pediatric checks

Title: Vital signs Observation conforms to pediatric vital-signs profile category
Preconditions: FHIR enabled; pediatric vitals recorded
Test_Steps: 1. GET vital-signs Observations | 2. Verify category=vital-signs and component coding
Expected_Result: Pediatric vitals carry category vital-signs, correct LOINC codes and UCUM units consistent with FHIR vital-signs profile

Title: BSA-based chemotherapy dose uses height and weight
Preconditions: Oncology pediatric patient with current height and weight; BSA dosing configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open BSA-dosed order | 2. System computes BSA from height/weight | 3. Verify dose
Test_Data: Height=120cm; Weight=23kg; Drug rate per m2
Expected_Result: System computes BSA (e.g., Mosteller) and multiplies by per-m2 rate; missing height blocks BSA dosing

Title: Percentile readout localization and decimal separator handling
Preconditions: System supports multiple locales
Test_Steps: 1. View percentile in locales with comma vs dot decimals | 2. Verify numeric formatting
Expected_Result: Percentile and z-score format per locale decimal separator without value corruption across locale pairs

Title: Newborn linked to mother's encounter for delivery context
Preconditions: Mother has a delivery encounter; newborn just registered
Test_Steps: 1. Register newborn | 2. Associate with mother delivery visit/encounter | 3. Verify linkage
Expected_Result: Newborn record is associable with the mother's delivery encounter context for continuity; link persists and is auditable
